title: 'Systemic exploitation': India urges world to hold Pak 'fully accountable' over PoK unrest
content: India stated Pakistan's exploitation causes protests in Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir. The ministry of external affairs spokesperson Randhir Jaiswal accused Islamabad of excessive force against public dissent. India maintains Pakistan-occupied Jammu and Kashmir is an integral part of India. New Delhi also accused Pakistan of human rights violations in the region.

title: Trump's 'guardian' fee: How much would supertanker have to pay to cross Hormuz
content: Unlike man-made canals such as the Suez and Panama, which are sovereign infrastructure and levy substantial transit tolls, most natural international waterways - including the Strait of Hormuz, Strait of Malacca, Taiwan Strait, Strait of Gibraltar and Cape of Good Hope—generally do not charge transit fees.

title: 'They chased us for 15 km': Daughter recounts assault on mother; 9 arrested in Nashik
content: What began as a peaceful family picnic near Bhavali Dam quickly escalated into a nightmare. Two young men harassed a woman, prompting her father to intervene. This led to a violent chase, where the family's SUV was followed and attacked by a gang on motorcycles. Allegations of molestation and assault surfaced, prompting police action that resulted in nine arrests after the family provided video evidence.

title: Jeff Bezos’ wife Lauren Sánchez leads $100M investment to transform 8 US cities
content: Lauren Sanchez Bezos is directing significant Bezos Earth Fund investments. The fund is transforming urban spaces into green community hubs. This initiative aims to connect people and nature within cities. The Bezos Earth Fund also supports marine protection and AI climate solutions. Overall, the fund continues its commitment to climate and nature initiatives.

title: OBC status of 77 castes: Suvendu govt withdraws Mamata-era SC challenge to HC verdict
content: West Bengal withdrew its Supreme Court appeal challenging the High Court's OBC list judgment. The state cabinet decided not to pursue the appeal, which was previously filed by the Trinamool Congress government. The Calcutta High Court had struck down the inclusion of 77 communities, including 75 Muslim communities. The state assembly has since passed two bills to restructure the OBC reservation framework.

title: MS Dhoni and Kriti Sanon steal the spotlight at India vs England ODI
content: MS Dhoni and Kriti Sanon attended the India versus England ODI match. England collapsed from a strong start to a low total. Jasprit Bumrah and Gurnoor Brar took key wickets. Joe Root and Liam Dawson led a spirited recovery for England. Axar Patel's late spell restricted England to 258 runs.

title: 'He said he'd be back next week': Kin shares victim's final call before Vietnam boat tragedy
content: Ravi Teja's family shared his final joyful video calls before the Vietnam boat tragedy. He enthusiastically showed his family scenic spots and the boat journey. His daughter recalled his promise to return next Wednesday for a program. His mother spoke with him for thirty minutes Saturday morning. Mortal remains of Ravi Teja and other victims were repatriated.

title: 'London Rule' that explains why AI researchers are leaving Google for Anthropic & OpenAI
content: Four senior AI researchers have left Google DeepMind in under two weeks, joining rivals Anthropic and OpenAI. The common thread isn't pay—it's London. UK non-compete rules, enforceable unlike in California, explain why Nobel laureate John Jumper won't start at Anthropic until next year. With compute shifting to Mountain View and pre-IPO equity on offer, Google's London research bench is quietly draining westward.

title: Govt releases first services production index; 14 sub-sectors post double-digit growth
content: India's government has launched a new Index of Services Production. Fourteen of nineteen formal services sub-sectors showed double-digit growth in April. Accommodation and food services expanded significantly, leading the growth figures. Air and railway transport were the only sub-sectors to record declines. This index will now be released monthly to track economic activity.

title: New Horizons travelled 3 billion miles to Pluto but could not stop there
content: NASA's New Horizons spacecraft completed its swift flyby of Pluto after a nine-year journey. The mission prioritized data collection over live communication during the brief encounter. Scientists received approximately 50 billion bits of data over fifteen months after the flyby. This slow data return revealed Pluto's complex and geologically active icy world. The mission highlighted the trade-off between reach and the patience required for discovery.

title: AI radar ‘Bear-dar’ detects polar bear mother and cubs in Canada
content: An AI radar system called Bear-dar successfully detected a polar bear family. This system alerted weather station staff in Eureka, Nunavut, allowing a safe response. The bears were guided away from the station and returned to their sea ice habitat. Bear-dar was developed to detect polar bears in all conditions and times. This technology aims to reduce human-wildlife encounters and improve safety for everyone.

title: Use AI or not? How dos and don’ts have changed for engineers
content: Tech firms are now focusing on productivity and business outcomes from AI. Companies previously encouraged or mandated AI tool usage for employees. This shift occurs as AI usage costs rise and businesses demand tangible returns. Employers are moving away from rewarding AI usage itself. Instead, they seek purposeful AI integration for measurable results.

title: 'Political conspiracy': Delhi CM blames AAP top brass over Tahir Hussain conviction
content: Rekha Gupta accused the Aam Aadmi Party of political patronage to Tahir Hussain. She demanded answers from the party leadership regarding their association with him. Hussain was convicted for his role in the 2020 Delhi riots and murder. The conviction brought justice to victims and their devastated families. The chief minister stressed accountability and punishment for those responsible.

title: 13 Indians killed, 3 missing in Gulf since February 28 amid US-Iran war
content: Thirteen Indians have died and three are missing in the Gulf region since late February. Recent attacks on commercial ships have resulted in significant Indian casualties. India is closely monitoring the escalating regional crisis and its impact. The nation calls for safe and uninterrupted navigation through the Strait of Hormuz. Attacks on shipping must cease to ensure global trade resumes.

title: Thane hospital assault case: Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre gets bail
content: A local court granted bail to Shiv Sena corporator Ramesh Mhatre on Tuesday. He was arrested for allegedly assaulting doctors and medical staff at a civic hospital. Mhatre and three associates were sent to judicial custody following the incident. The alleged assault triggered widespread outrage across Maharashtra after a video went viral. The two doctors Mhatre is accused of assaulting have since submitted their resignations.

title: Beyond the border: How India is building firepower to strike targets 1,000km away
content: India's military is enhancing long-range strike capabilities for future conflicts. New weapons and drone technology are increasing engagement distances significantly. These advancements allow for precise strikes deep within enemy territory. India aims to control escalation and dictate conflict resolution terms. This modernization drive ensures a continued combat edge in the region.

title: All not well between Gambhir & Agarkar? Dinesh Karthik drops 'friction' bombshell
content: Dinesh Karthik suggests friction between Gautam Gambhir and Ajit Agarkar over selection priorities.  India's recent T20I series losses in the UK have intensified scrutiny on the team's management.  Karthik believes differing long-term plans and immediate winning desires cause selection confusion.  The immense squad depth adds pressure on players struggling to secure their places.  The BCCI will review these poor T20I performances and selections soon.

title: World Cup Semi-Final: Why England vs Argentina is more 'war' than football
content: A World Cup semi-final brings together two nations separated by an ocean and united by six decades of grudges, ghosts and glorious bad blood.

title: Brothers detained by Pak forces found dead in Balochistan; custody death concerns raised
content: Two brothers, Imam and Muhammad Umar, were found dead with bullet injuries in Balochistan.  They were allegedly taken into custody by security personnel after their motorcycle ran out of fuel.  Their family later received their bodies, disputing claims of militant links.  Local residents expressed concern over the circumstances surrounding their deaths.  No official response has been issued by Pakistan's military or government.

title: Not ‘treasured’! Why India, China are stacking up gold & trimming US Treasuries exposure
content: India is among major economies that are stepping up purchases of gold, while trimming holdings of US Treasuries. Central banks have increasingly begun viewing gold not only as a hedge against inflation but also as a core store of value.

title: Two things my DNA told me that eleven years of blood tests never could
content: Genetic sequencing revealed unique responses to rosuvastatin medication for Nickhil Jakatdar. His body processes the statin more slowly than average, influencing future treatment decisions. A variant associated with Gilbert syndrome was also identified through his genome. This finding indicated that prolonged intermittent fasting might not be universally beneficial for him. These nuanced genetic insights provided confidence to tailor his health approach.

title: 'Had engine problem': Vlogger Sourav Joshi backtracks on E20 petrol mileage claim
content: YouTuber Sourav Joshi clarified his Mercedes-Benz SUV mileage concerns after switching to E20 petrol. Mercedes-Benz India stated all its BS VI petrol vehicles are compatible with E20 fuel. Joshi later explained an engine issue caused the low mileage, not the fuel itself. He apologized for any misunderstanding and trimmed the controversial vlog segment. The company reiterated its commitment to customer safety and sustainable mobility.

title: From Rahul to Vijay to Nadda: CJP seeks support for NEET protesters
content: The Cockroach Janta Party has invited political leaders to join a protest at Jantar Mantar. This demonstration supports students protesting the alleged NEET UG examination irregularities and paper leak. Leaders from various parties have been urged to stand in solidarity with the protesting youth. The organization seeks accountability and justice for students affected by the education system. Many leaders have already offered support, while others are still being contacted.

title: Buying a home in Delhi-NCT? Here's how to register your property
content: Proper property registration is essential to validate ownership and maintain a public record. When it comes to sale deeds, applicable stamp duty and registration fees are determined by the sale's value. Although will registration is not mandatory, it reinforces ownership claims and minimises disputes. Furthermore, registration charges apply to gift deeds and share certificates, ensuring that buyers' rights are safeguarded against potential legal challenges.

title: Jasprit Bumrah scripts history, surpasses Ravindra Jadeja to become ...
content: Bumrah reached the milestone in the opening ball of the 14th over of England's innings. He forced Brook into a tentative shot with a slower delivery that climbed on the batter, and Rohit Sharma completed a catch at first slip. The wicket was also Bumrah's 150th in ODI cricket.

title: 'Learning a language never goes waste': SC declines to stay CBSE's 3-language policy
content: The Supreme Court declined to halt the CBSE's three-language policy for 2026-27. It will hear detailed challenges to the policy next week. Petitioners argued against the policy's implementation and lack of resources. The court noted that learning languages is beneficial and agreed to hear arguments. The Union government was directed to file its response within ten days.

title: Jammu court summons crime branch officer in Rs 500-crore Vaishno Devi row
content: A Jammu court has ordered an inquiry officer to appear with records concerning alleged fake silver offerings. Approximately 20 tonnes of silver offerings were reportedly found to be mostly inferior metals. An advocate filed a complaint seeking an FIR and investigation into the alleged misappropriation. The court directed the inquiry officer to present relevant records on July 29, 2026.

title: 'Vessels carried 30 Indian seafarers': MEA condemns Hormuz attack that killed sailor
content: India registered a strong protest against continued attacks on vessels carrying Indian seafarers. Two vessels, MT Al Bahiyah and MT Mombasa, were attacked in the Strait of Hormuz. One Indian national tragically lost his life while others sustained injuries during the attacks. The ministry summoned Iran's deputy chief of mission to lodge a strong protest. India calls for an immediate cessation of violence and a return to dialogue.

title: Weather tomorrow: IMD warns of extremely heavy rain in 14 states
content: Northeast and East India will experience extremely heavy rainfall over the next week. Southern and coastal states are expected to face continued hot and humid weather conditions. Uttarakhand and parts of North India will also receive heavy rainfall starting mid-July. Telangana authorities are preparing for potential El Niño impacts on agriculture. The monsoon remains active over eastern regions, bringing significant precipitation.

title: NHL Trade Rumors: Maple Leafs urged to acquire $92.8M forward for Cup run
content: Trade speculation surrounding Elias Pettersson continues to grow as the Toronto Maple Leafs search for another top-six center. Although his eight-year, $92.8 million contract presents a major cap challenge, reports suggest the Vancouver Canucks are open to moving him for a relatively modest return. Analysts believe Toronto could benefit from placing Pettersson behind Auston Matthews while shifting John Tavares into a more suitable role. Whether the financial pieces can be aligned remains the biggest question, but this is one of the most intriguing NHL trade rumors to watch this offseason.

title: Expired Maggi sold by Vishal Mega Mart leaves man ill; court imposes Rs 2.8 lakh penalty
content: A consumer commission found Vishal Mega Mart liable for selling expired noodles. The customer suffered food poisoning after consuming the product purchased in March 2025. The store failed to appear before the commission to contest the allegations. Vishal Mega Mart must pay compensation and punitive damages to the affected consumer. The commission also ordered a deposit into the Chief Minister's Relief Fund.

title: Jos Buttler enters elite club, becomes only the second England player to...
content: Jos Buttler achieved his 200th One-Day International cap for England. He reached this significant milestone during the opening ODI against India. Former captain Eoin Morgan holds the record for most ODIs played. England batted first after winning the toss at Edgbaston. India welcomed back senior players like Virat Kohli and Rohit Sharma.

title: 'Sensitive matter': SC orders separate namaz space near Bhojshala; flags MP govt's lax law & order
content: The Supreme Court directed that any ASI structural alterations require its prior permission in the Bhojshala row. Separate space for Friday prayers for Muslims adjacent to the site was also directed. Both Hindu and Muslim sides were urged to exercise restraint during proceedings.

title: 'Dil mein Babar, munh mein Ram': Anti-SP posters ignite fresh political storm in UP
content: Posters targeting Mulayam Singh Yadav and Akhilesh Yadav appeared across Uttar Pradesh. The Samajwadi Party accused the BJP of inciting communal sentiments with these displays. Uttar Pradesh minister Om Prakash Rajbhar defended the posters as appropriate responses. Samajwadi Party workers removed the controversial hoardings in several locations. Police are examining CCTV footage to identify those responsible for the posters.

title: India tour of Bangladesh gets closer to approval; Afghanistan T20Is could be pushed back
content: India's white-ball tour of Bangladesh is edging closer to approval, with the BCCI exploring a revised international schedule. To accommodate the six-match series, India's proposed T20Is against Afghanistan are likely to be pushed back by a few days, while the BCB remains optimistic about hosting the Men in Blue after a four-year gap.

title: 'Dhamaal 4' BO Day 5 [LIVE]: Inches towards Rs 80 crore on first Tuesday
content: 'Dhamaal 4', led by Ajay Devgn, Arshad Warsi, Riteish Deshmukh and Jaaved Jaaferi, is closing out its opening week at the box office. After a stellar Day 3 that brought in Rs 28.50 crore, the film saw a sharp 69% drop on Day 4, and is now tracking modest numbers on Day 5 as it heads toward the Rs 75 crore domestic net mark.

title: UK seeks law change to deport Pakistani rapist and grooming gang leader
content: The UK government is changing a law that prevents serious criminals' deportation.  Home Secretary Shabana Mahmood announced Parliament will amend the Immigration Act 1971.  This change targets individuals like grooming gang leader Shabir Ahmed.  Ahmed was jailed for rape and sexual offenses against young girls.  The government seeks to remove legal protections for such serious offenders.

title: Trump tariffs struck down by US Supreme Court: How much has been refunded so far
content: The Supreme Court's recent decision led the US government to refund a staggering eighty-one billion dollars in tariffs, overturning many of President Donald Trump's imposed tariffs. This fiscal year has already seen a notable surge in refunds according to Treasury budget data. A ruling from the US Court of International Trade in May also challenged these blanket tariffs.

title: Kargil War: How July 14 turned the tide and put Pakistan on back foot
content: The Indian Army marked July 14, 1999, as a decisive turning point in the Kargil War. This date saw India regain control of strategic heights and Pakistan announce troop withdrawal. The Fire and Fury Corps highlighted exceptional courage and coordinated operations during this period. Prime Minister Atal Bihari Vajpayee declared Operation Vijay a success on this significant day.

title: Shah Rukh Khan gets relief as SC clears way for Mannat's two additional floors
content: The Supreme Court has dismissed a petition challenging the Coastal Regulation Zone clearance. This ruling allows Shah Rukh Khan to proceed with renovations at his Mumbai home. The apex court declined to interfere with an earlier National Green Tribunal order. Activist Santosh Daundkar's appeal was rejected by the bench. The legal dispute over Mannat's expansion has now concluded.

title: 'Zero Occupants, Regular Funding': Auditor Flags Ghost Hostels In Maharashtra
content: The findings are part of the Compliance Audit Report 2024, tabled in the Maharashtra Assembly during the ongoing monsoon session.

title: PM Modi Stopped Putin From Nuking Ukraine In 2022: Polish Minister
content: Poland's minister referred to developments in late 2022, claiming that PM Modi had played a role in persuading Russia against using tactical nuclear weapons during the war.

title: German Shepherd Fights Off Leopard In Gujarat, Saves 3 Men Before Dying
content: The incident happened when the leopard attacked three people sleeping on cots outside a local restuarant.

title: US, Iran Intensify Strikes After Trump Reveals Hormuz Toll Plan
content: The US launched strikes on Iran early Tuesday, hours after President Donald Trump vowed to reinstate an American blockade of Iranian ports.

title: Tests Found Aftab Poonawala Had "Psycho, Sociopathic" Tendencies: Sources
content: Shraddha Walkar murder case: During the assessments, Aftab Poonawala did not show any remorse for the crime, sources said

title: Bloomberg Ordered To Pay $178,000 In Singapore Ministers' Defamation Case
content: Bloomberg Editor-in-Chief John Micklethwait said the agency was disappointed by the ruling "but we will of course respect it".

title: EU Lawmakers Demand "Non-Addictive Youth Mode" On Social Media
content: The EU has increased pressure on social media platforms to change recently, telling Facebook, TikTok and Instagram to dismantle "addictive" features.

title: "Hostile Act": Iran Slams UK Move To Ban Support For Revolutionary Guards
content: British Prime Minister Keir Starmer had said on Monday that "anyone found supporting or assisting these groups will now face up to 14 years in prison".

title: Woman Alleges TVK Leader Harassed Her For Months. Her Relatives Thrash Him
content: The married woman, who lives in Malaiyappa Nagar in Kulithalai, filed a police complaint stating that the TVK Krishnarayapuram East union secretary had harassed and tried to kidnap her.

title: Water-Based Infiltration, Martial Arts, AI: Lashkar's 'Upgrades' Add To India's Worries
content: Some of the training is taking place at the Mangla Dam, where Ajmal Kasab and other attackers had also trained.

title: 6 Civilians Killed After Pakistan Forces Open Fire During Clashes In PoK
content: Among those killed in Baloch Sadhunati district were Zahid Mughal, Zafar Mughal and Arsalan Akbar. The latest violence has further intensified tensions in the region, where resentment against Islamabad has been steadily increasing.

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: Iran Authorities Say US Struck Port City Of Bushehr
content: US-Iran War Live Updates: The flare-up is the latest to undermine an interim agreement between Washington and Tehran aimed at ending their war, which has caused global economic shockwaves since it began in late February.

title: US Journalists Flagged Air Force One 'Gaps'. Can They Be Forced To Testify?
content: US Attorney Jay Clayton issued the subpoenas ordering journalists to testify on Wednesday concerning "alleged violation of federal criminal law."

title: Opinion: The Politics Of Satluj: A Film That No Party Can Fully Own
content: Satluj has already achieved one political outcome: it has forced every major party to revisit questions that have remained politically unresolved for four decades

title: PM Modi To Launch Rs 2,500-Crore Projects In Punjab On Friday
content: These healthcare projects worth nearly Rs 1,200 crore will help to further advanced medical treatment, research and education in northern India.

title: Stock Market Highlights, Sensex Today: Sensex Closes 560 Points Lower As Oil Prices Rally Amid US-Iran Tensions
content: Stock Market, Sensex, Share Market, Nifty Highlights: Crude oil prices jump to one-month high after 10% rally; Brent oil near $85 a barrel.

title: Delhi's New Policy Paves Way For High-Rises. Architects Flag One Big Risk
content: Without parallel investments in roads and urban design, higher density could simply place more pressure on already strained neighbourhoods.

title: Gang of five habitual offenders held for impersonating CCB officers, kidnapping gym trainer
content: According to the complaint, the victim, Mithul Kunder, 25, a gym trainer from J.P. Nagar, had gone to a club on the evening of June 19 to play sports.

title: Bengaluru: Police bust ₹100-crore land grab attempt; 11 arrested for forging records
content: The accused allegedly created fake genealogy records, death certificates, grant certificates and other revenue documents to falsely establish ownership over six acres of the trust’s land.

title: Guntur neurologist elected to World Stroke Organisation Board
content: Dr. P. Vijaya secures one of the 18 director positions through a global election involving 3,500 members from 95 countries; she will serve a four-year term from 2026 to 2030

title: Hyderabad water board readies emergency plan as Singur, Manjira inflows remain uncertain
content: Plan should assess reservoir-wise water availability, minimum drinking water requirements, potential supply deficits and the feasibility of diverting water from the Krishna, Godavari and Mission Bhagiratha systems, says Water Board MD

title: Can’t English be considered an indigenous Indian language, asks SC
content: SC questions the use of the colonially loaded term ‘native’ in CBSE circular, says the push for three-language scheme may be in the spirit of the Constitutional goal to use Indian languages for official purposes

title: Develop Mamnoor, Adilabad airports to international standards, CM urges Centre
content: Revanth Reddy requests Union Civil Aviation Minister K. Ramamohan Naidu to examine the possibility of operating seaplane services between Prakasam Barrage in Vijayawada and Hussainsagar;  no link between politics and development - politics only during elections, says Chief Minister Not appropriate to criticise the State for consulting Centre to resolve problems and speed up development

title: Officials, contractors told to complete all pending FSTP, STP works within stipulated time
content: Deputy Commissioner chairs review meeting on drinking water supply project under Amrut 2.0 and sewerage works at her office in Hosapete

title: Congress expresses concern over resumption of hostilities in West Asia, urges Centre to protect India’s interests
content: Flagging Pakistan’s expanding role in West Asia, the Opposition party says the neighbouring country’s  growing influence in West Asia is a setback for India

title: Bidadi Township Project gathers wider social, political base
content: While a forceful push to acquire land could alienate farmers and rural voters, abandoning or scaling down the project could weaken the government’s development narrative and be seen as yielding to Opposition-backed protests

title: TTD Board approves revised donor policy, major pilgrim projects
content: Board clears infrastructure works, appoints 791 honorary Vedic scholars, approves green energy initiative and new facilities for devotees across Tirumala and Ontimitta.

title: PM Modi to inaugurate renovated Mangalagiri railway station on July 17
content: The station has been modernised as part of the Centre's Amrit Bharat Station Scheme (ABSS) at a cost of ₹12.56 crore with upgraded passenger amenities and improved infrastructure

title: CJP protest Day 25: As Sonam Wangchuk’s condition deteriorates, calls to end fast pour in
content: Over 1,800 “writers, academics, social activists and other concerned citizens” signed on to an online petition asking Mr. Wangchuk to end his fast.

title: BJP seeks apology from Kejriwal, Sonia, and Rahul after Tahir Hussain conviction
content: After Tahir Hussain’s conviction in the Ankit Sharma murder case linked to the 2020 North-East Delhi riots, the BJP accuses Opposition leaders of shielding the accused and demands an unconditional apology

title: EC extends SIR schedule for Haryana, Andhra Pradesh
content: The two States are part of Phase 3 of the SIR rolled out on May 14, which is covering 16 States and Union Territories

title: Leak in gas pipeline during roadwork in Kerala capital sparks panic
content: Leak occurrs on KIMS Road at Kumarapuram. It was soon brought under control. Officials confirm that there is presently no danger to the public

title: Railway Minister announces reforms to cut red tape, boost freight, standardise skilling
content: The reforms also ease regulatory requirements for container train operators and private manufacturers

title: Police sub-inspector accused of dowry harassment, assault; arrested
content: His wife alleged that despite approaching Kengeri Police Station, her complaint was not registered as an FIR, and was instead reduced to an NCR (Non-Cognizable Report)

title: Bengaluru: Stakeholders’ meeting on Karnataka’s Apartment Bill to be held on July 15
content: The apartment owners have been urging the government for the enactment of the Bill to bring more clarity regarding ownership and management of apartments in the State.

title: Opposition spreading false information about fraudulent registration of Palani mutt land, says HR & CE Minister Ramesh
content: The Minister accuses the Opposition parties of linking him to individuals who had fraudulently registered 1.4 acres of land at the foothills of Palani belonging to the Dhandayuthapani Swami Mutt. He says the Sub-Registrar (in-charge), who registered the document on July 6, 2026, has been placed under suspension

title: Remains of three Andhra tourists killed in Vietnam boat accident brought home
content: Minister Kondapalli Srinivas stayed at the Shamshabad airport through Monday night, receiving the remains flown in via Mumbai and consoling the families before the bodies were sent to Kadapa, Machilipatnam and Hindupur

title: VACB questions Swapna Suresh in Life Mission bribery case
content: The questioning comes as the VACB prepares to submit its chargesheet in a case that involves allegations of kickbacks related to the government housing project intended for flood victims.

title: Bengaluru City Police Commissionerate to be split into multiple units?
content: While all other major city regions in India — Delhi, Mumbai, Kolkata, Hyderabad and Chennai — have multiple commissionerates, Bengaluru remains the only one where a large commissionerate, including suburban areas, is surrounded by rural district police units.

title: To AIADMK, DMK remains an evil force, clarifies Palaniswami
content: His remarks come amid allegations that the DMK and the AIADMK had attempted to join hands to prevent the TVK from forming a government after the recent election threw up a hung Assembly

title: El Niño effect: Spices Board confirms dip in cardamom production in Idukki
content: According to IMD data, the normal rainfall in Idukki during the monsoon season up to July 14 is 1,096.6 mm, but the district received only 492 mm

title: Software testing firm TestingXperts opens 300-seater delivery centre in Hyderabad
content: 200 people onboarded; number likely to be 500 within a year; long-term plan is for a 2,500-seat campus in Hyderabad, TestingXperts leadership says

title: Rajnath Singh flags off Delhi-Kargil ‘Shaurya Vijay Yatra’ motorcycle rally
content: The expedition, with the theme of ‘One Ride, One Nation, One Salute’, is part of the nationwide observance of Kargil Vijay Diwas 2026; Indian soldiers achieved the impossible in Operation Vijay, Defence Minister says

title: Silk saree that fits into a matchbox presented to Goddess Bharamaraba
content: The saree measures 5.5 metres in length and 48 inches in width and weighs approximately 200 grams; designed in the traditional “Ikkat” pattern, the saree can easily fit into a small matchbox when folded, the weaver explained

title: SC says call records alone not cogent proof, clears woman of husband’s murder
content: Mere production of telephone records does not substitute substantive proof of an illicit affair leading to murder, the top court said in the case dating back to 2007

title: Kerala BJP councillor detained under KAAPA retakes oath inside prison in unprecedented move
content: Kerala High Court earlier permitted KAAPA detainee R. Sugathan to take oath in Viyyur jail; political row erupts over unprecedented ceremony

title: Munnar DFO to issue final order on seizure of earth mover of tribal man within one week
content: DFO Saju Varghese says proceedings under Section 61A of Forest Conservation Act will be completed within one week; owner of vehicle says Forest department did not provide any assurance during meeting regarding its return

title: Bankipur bypoll: Prashant Kishor declares Rs 96 cr assets; wife owns Rs 101.93 cr
